quiz.title: Algebra 2: Unit 10 Quiz 1
quiz.subtitle: 10.1: Measures of Central Tendency and Dispersion
quiz.subject: Mathematics
quiz.topic: Calculating and interpreting mean, median, mode, and standard deviation

questions.question: A data set consists of the following values: $12, 15, 15, 18, 20, 22, 30$. What is the mean of this data set?
A student's grade is weighted: homework (20%, average 85), quizzes (30%, average 72), tests (50%, average 91). Find the overall weighted average.
A data set has standard deviation $4$. If each value is multiplied by $3$, what is the new standard deviation?
questions.explanation: Multiplying all values by a constant multiplies the standard deviation by the absolute value of that constant: $4 \times 3 = 12$. Adding a constant doesn't change $\sigma$; multiplying does.

questions.question: A set of exam scores has a mean of $80$ and a standard deviation of $5$. What is the variance ($s^2$) of the scores?
questions.explanation: Variance is the square of the standard deviation: $5^2 = 25$.

questions.question: Find the median of the following data set: $4, 8, 1, 10, 12, 10, 7$.
questions.explanation: Order the data: $1, 4, 7, 8, 10, 10, 12$. With $n=7$, the median is the 4th value: $8$.

questions.question: Find the population standard deviation of: $2, 4, 4, 4, 5, 5, 7, 9$.
A data set contains the values $15, 15, 16, 18$, and $40$. What is the mode of this data?
questions.explanation: The mode is the most frequent value. $15$ appears twice; all others appear once. Mode = $15$.

questions.question: Data set A: $5, 5, 5, 5, 5$. Data set B: $1, 3, 5, 7, 9$. Find the standard deviation of each.
questions.explanation: Set A: no variation → $\sigma = 0$. Set B: mean = $5$, deviations $-4,-2,0,2,4$, squared sum = $40$, $\sigma = \sqrt{40/5} = \sqrt{8} \approx 2.83$.

questions.question: If the sum of squares of the deviations from the mean is 200 for a population of $n=8$, what is the population standard deviation ($\sigma$)?
The range of a data set is 50. If the lowest value is 12, what is the highest value?

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You have a strong grasp of central tendency and dispersion — mean, weighted mean, median, mode, range, variance, and standard deviation including the effect of transformations.
scoreMessages.70: Good job. Key reminders: (1) Weighted mean: multiply each value by its weight and sum, (2) Multiplying data by a constant multiplies $\sigma$ by that constant; adding a constant doesn't change $\sigma$, (3) Variance = $\sigma^2$, (4) Standard deviation = $\sqrt{\frac{\sum(x-\bar{x})^2}{n}}$ for a population.
scoreMessages.50: Keep practicing. Focus on: (1) Ordering data before finding the median, (2) Computing deviations, squaring them, summing, dividing by $n$, then taking the square root, (3) The mode is the most frequent value — a set can have one, more than one, or none.
scoreMessages.0: Review measures of central tendency: (1) Mean = sum / count, (2) Median = middle value when ordered, (3) Mode = most frequent value, (4) Standard deviation = $\sqrt{\frac{\sum(x-\bar{x})^2}{n}}$, (5) Range = max $-$ min, (6) Variance = $\sigma^2$.
